title: Raid on the Jedi Temple (Yinchorri Uprising)

{{Quote|Find the young ones first, kill all the Jedi children!|A [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] commander|<ref name="Jedi Council: Acts of War 2">[[Jedi Council: Acts of War 2|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'' 2]]</ref>}}
In [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], the [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] '''raided''' the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] under the influence of [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]].

==Background==
{{Quote|I convinced clans was great time to go kill younglings at temple. Yinchorri big on that. Kill enemy is good. Kill enemy's offspring is better. So they go do that.|[[Vilmarh Grahrk]]|<ref name="Jedi Council: Acts of War">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Council: Acts of War]]''</ref>}}
Following the dispatchment of a task force of Jedi to the Yinchorri system to investigate reports of Yinchorri aggression, reports came of their encounter with Yinchorri forces during a briefing of the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]. However, the messages were confusing and contradictory. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] also sensed danger was coming through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].

==The battle==
[[File:Darth_Maul_Yinchorri.jpg|thumb|left|[[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] overlooking the raid.]]

Outside the Jedi Temple, the Yinchorri were in position and ready for attack. All of this was being watched from a nearby skyscraper by Darth Sidious' apprentice [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]]. He informed his master that the Yinchorri had begun their attack.

Meanwhile, outside the Temple, the Jedi [[Jude Rozess]] was on guard duty when she was suddenly attacked by a Yinchorri donning a [[Yinchorri flutter-pack|flutter-pack]]. Jude fought him, though her lightsaber was disabled by his powerful cortosis shield. Having immobilized her, he told his comrades to attack before the Jedi were aware of their presence while he attempted to kill Jude. He gripped her with his massive hands and strangled her. However, Jude also killed him by reactivating her lightsaber.

Back in the Temple, the Yinchorri task force found that all the sleeping quarters were empty. The troops then entered a chamber where they were met by several armed Jedi, including Yoda. In the end, almost all the Yinchorri were killed while only two Jedi died: Jude Rozess and [[Tieren Nie-Tan]].

[[File:Yoda_Yinchorri.jpg|left|thumb|[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] smashing a [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] against the wall.]]

Yoda interrogated the last remaining Yinchorri and asked him the location of the Yinchorri high command. The soldier responded to the Jedi Master by telling him that he did not know since he was only an ordinary soldier. Yoda then turned to converse with [[Soon Bayts]], a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who brought word from [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]] and [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]] that their high command was not on [[Yitheeth]] and that they were heading to [[Yibikkoror]] to help [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] and [[Micah Giiett]]. Meanwhile on [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]], Mace Windu's team was safe and they did not engage the Yinchorri in combat at the time.

These distractions gave the Yinchorri in the temple enough time to grab his weapon and attempt to shoot the Jedi. Yoda threw him against a wall, presumably killing him. The Jedi then came to the conclusion that the Yinchorri were misguided rather than bloodthirsty and that the attack may have been the work of an unknown party, rather than the Yinchorri themselves.
